events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["non-compliant","installed-defective"],"percent_complete":70,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Emergency lighting fixture is wired on a switched leg, causing emergency egress illumination to be controlled by a general lighting switch, violating NEC 700 requirements for independent emergency power and NFPA 101 egress illumination requirements."],"actions":["Re-wire emergency fixture to unswitched emergency power circuit supplied by emergency source; remove from switched leg; verify emergency illumination operates upon loss of normal power."],"rationale":"Emergency egress lighting must remain energized or transfer to emergency power independently of general lighting controls. Placing an emergency fixture on a switched leg means egress illumination can be turned off, violating NEC 700 and NFPA 101.","references":["NEC 700","NFPA 101"]}
